title: Anthropic Buys Biology - TCR 04/04/26
description:
Anthropic acquired a drug discovery startup for $400 million, Microsoft released three in-house AI models built without OpenAI, BYD launched a $22,000 EV with five-minute charging that accumulated 37,000 orders in a month, and renewable energy records fell across six U.S. grid regions.

https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-april-4-2026/

Anthropic's $400 million stock acquisition of Coefficient Bio brings computational drug discovery directly inside a frontier AI company, extending the pattern of structural integration between AI labs and the pharmaceutical industry. Microsoft released MAI-Transcribe-1, MAI-Voice-1, and MAI-Image-2 through Foundry - three models built by Mustafa Suleyman's team that carry no OpenAI branding, with the transcription model outperforming Whisper across all 25 languages tested. A data breach at training vendor Mercor exposed proprietary datasets from multiple frontier labs through a single compromised open-source dependency, prompting Meta to pause all work with the company. A University of Pennsylvania study formalized the concept of "cognitive surrender," finding that large majorities of AI users accept outputs without critical evaluation - published on the same day Utah launched a pilot delegating psychiatric medication renewals to an AI system. Wind, solar, and battery records broke across six U.S. grid regions this spring, and BYD launched the Song Ultra EV at $22,000 with five-minute Flash Charging, accumulating 37,000 orders as U.S. gas prices passed $4.09 per gallon.

Read the full edition: https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-april-4-2026/
